lede: A look at October 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Amon-Ra St. Brown and Charlie White (figure skater), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hong Kong–Zhuhai–Macau Bridge
events.desc: The world's longest sea crossing, the Hong Kong–Zhuhai–Macau Bridge, opens for public traffic.

events.title: Nedelin catastrophe
events.desc: Nedelin catastrophe: An R-16 ballistic missile explodes on the launch pad at the Soviet Union's Baikonur Cosmodrome space facility, killing over 100 people, including Field Marshal Mitrofan Nedelin.

events.title: Headquarters of the United Nations
events.desc: The cornerstone of the United Nations Headquarters is laid.

events.title: Charter of the United Nations
events.desc: The United Nations Charter comes into effect.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Japan's center force is temporarily repulsed in the Battle of Leyte Gulf.

events.title: George Washington Bridge
events.desc: The George Washington Bridge opens to public traffic over the Hudson River.

events.title: Wall Street crash of 1929
events.desc: "Black Thursday" on the New York Stock Exchange, the first day of the panic in the markets that contributes to the Great Depression.

events.title: Danish West Indies
events.desc: U.S. Government announces plans to buy Danish West Indies for $7 million.

events.title: Henry Parkes
events.desc: Henry Parkes delivers the Tenterfield Oration, effectively starting the federation process in Australia.

events.title: William Lassell
events.desc: William Lassell discovers the moons Umbriel and Ariel orbiting Uranus.

births.name: Pablove Black
births.line: Jamaican singer-songwriter, keyboard player, and producer

births.name: Bill Wyman
births.line: English singer-songwriter, bass player, and producer

deaths.text: Lew Mayne, American football player and coach
İsmail Erez, Turkish lawyer and diplomat, Turkish Ambassador to France

observances.text: Christian feast day: Five Martyrs of Carthage (Felix and Companions)
Christian feast day: Rafael Guízar y Valencia
